{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Video Editor",
  "description": "Mshel Homes Limited is a real estate development and marketing company incorporated in 2018 with a unique vision to be the leading real estate company in Nigeria, with an outstanding reputation for honesty, integrity and impeccable customer service.


At Mshel Homes Limited our mission is to create a lifetime relationship with our clientele by deliverying property solutions through innovative and technological driven processes that caters to your real estate needs whilst adhering to the highest standards, systems and performance goals necessary to achieving your dreams.



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Video Editor

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time



Job Summary


	We are seeking a meticulous and creative Video Editor to join our production team. The ideal candidate masters the art of &quot;invisible editing&quot;&mdash;seamlessly manipulating and assembling film pieces so the audience remains immersed in the story without noticing the technical work behind it.
	You will bridge the gap between production and post-production, ensuring continuity and narrative flow from raw script to final cut.


Key Responsibilities


	Invisible Storytelling: Manipulate and edit filmed footage in a way that feels natural and immersive, ensuring the editing work is invisible to the audience and serves the narrative.
	Assembly &amp; Sequencing: Review shooting scripts and raw material to create a shot decision list based on each scene&rsquo;s value and its contribution to overall continuity.
	Rough to Final Cuts: Trim footage segments, assemble the sequence of the film, and iteratively develop projects from rough cuts to final, polished deliverables.
	Audio &amp; Visual Enhancement: Impute and synchronize music, dialogues, sound effects, and graphics. Perform color correction and apply visual effects as needed.
	Interpret Creative Briefs: Take detailed briefs from the production team and stakeholders to fully grasp project needs, specifications, and desired outcomes.
	Cross-Departmental Consultation: Consult with stakeholders throughout production and post-production processes to align on creative direction and technical requirements.
	Cross-Platform Output: Work closely with other creative team members to repurpose and produce content for the company&rsquo;s website, social media pages (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube), and other platforms as required.
	Quality Control: Diligently review final productions for errors in audio, visual, and narrative flow, ensuring that all final edits reflect the company&rsquo;s brand identity and technical specifications.
	Technical Innovation: Continuously discover and implement new editing technologies, software updates, and industry best practices to maximize workflow efficiency and creative output.
	Asset Management: Organize and maintain project files, raw footage, and archives for easy retrieval and future use.


Qualifications
Required Skills &amp; Experience:


	Editing Mastery: Proven experience as a Video Editor with a strong portfolio demonstrating narrative flow, pacing, and &quot;invisible&quot; continuity editing.
	Software Proficiency: Advanced knowledge of industry-standard editing software (Adobe Premiere Pro or Final Cut Pro).
	Motion Graphics: Working knowledge of Adobe After Effects for basic motion graphics and title creation.
	Audio Engineering: Experience with audio mixing and cleaning (Adobe Audition, Audacity, or Logic Pro).
	Script-to-Screen Awareness: Ability to review a script and raw footage to determine the best shots for continuity and emotional impact.
	Experience with color grading software (DaVinci Resolve).
	Knowledge of compression standards and export settings for various digital platforms.
	Background in filming or cinematography.




Salary and Benefits


	N200,000 - N300,000 / Month and performance-based incentives.
	Opportunities for professional growth and development.
	A collaborative and innovative work environment.
	The chance to make a meaningful impact within the real estate industry.




Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to: hr@mshelhomeslagos.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Video Editor Job at Mshel Homes Limited




        
    

        
        Mshel Homes Limited is a real estate development and marketing company incorporated in 2018 with a unique vision to be the leading real estate company in Nigeria, with an outstanding reputation for honesty, integrity and impeccable customer service.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




At Mshel Homes Limited our mission is to create a lifetime relationship with our clientele by deliverying property solutions through innovative and technological driven processes that caters to your real estate needs whilst adhering to the highest standards, systems and performance goals necessary to achieving your dreams.



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Video Editor

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time



Job Summary


	We are seeking a meticulous and creative Video Editor to join our production team. The ideal candidate masters the art of "invisible editing"—seamlessly manipulating and assembling film pieces so the audience remains immersed in the story without noticing the technical work behind it.
	You will bridge the gap between production and post-production, ensuring continuity and narrative flow from raw script to final cut.


Key Responsibilities


	Invisible Storytelling: Manipulate and edit filmed footage in a way that feels natural and immersive, ensuring the editing work is invisible to the audience and serves the narrative.
	Assembly & Sequencing: Review shooting scripts and raw material to create a shot decision list based on each scene’s value and its contribution to overall continuity.
	Rough to Final Cuts: Trim footage segments, assemble the sequence of the film, and iteratively develop projects from rough cuts to final, polished deliverables.
	Audio & Visual Enhancement: Impute and synchronize music, dialogues, sound effects, and graphics. Perform color correction and apply visual effects as needed.
	Interpret Creative Briefs: Take detailed briefs from the production team and stakeholders to fully grasp project needs, specifications, and desired outcomes.
	Cross-Departmental Consultation: Consult with stakeholders throughout production and post-production processes to align on creative direction and technical requirements.
	Cross-Platform Output: Work closely with other creative team members to repurpose and produce content for the company’s website, social media pages (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube), and other platforms as required.
	Quality Control: Diligently review final productions for errors in audio, visual, and narrative flow, ensuring that all final edits reflect the company’s brand identity and technical specifications.
	Technical Innovation: Continuously discover and implement new editing technologies, software updates, and industry best practices to maximize workflow efficiency and creative output.
	Asset Management: Organize and maintain project files, raw footage, and archives for easy retrieval and future use.


Qualifications
Required Skills & Experience:


	Editing Mastery: Proven experience as a Video Editor with a strong portfolio demonstrating narrative flow, pacing, and "invisible" continuity editing.
	Software Proficiency: Advanced knowledge of industry-standard editing software (Adobe Premiere Pro or Final Cut Pro).
	Motion Graphics: Working knowledge of Adobe After Effects for basic motion graphics and title creation.
	Audio Engineering: Experience with audio mixing and cleaning (Adobe Audition, Audacity, or Logic Pro).
	Script-to-Screen Awareness: Ability to review a script and raw footage to determine the best shots for continuity and emotional impact.
	Experience with color grading software (DaVinci Resolve).
	Knowledge of compression standards and export settings for various digital platforms.
	Background in filming or cinematography.




Salary and Benefits


	N200,000 - N300,000 / Month and performance-based incentives.
	Opportunities for professional growth and development.
	A collaborative and innovative work environment.
	The chance to make a meaningful impact within the real estate industry.




Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Structural Design Engineer",
  "description": "Mshel Homes Limited is a real estate development and marketing company incorporated in 2018 with a unique vision to be the leading real estate company in Nigeria, with an outstanding reputation for honesty, integrity and impeccable customer service.

At Mshel Homes Limited our mission is to create a lifetime relationship with our clientele by deliverying property solutions through innovative and technological driven processes that caters to your real estate needs whilst adhering to the highest standards, systems and performance goals necessary to achieving your dreams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Structural Design Engineer

Job Location: Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced Structural Design Engineer to join our team. The Structural Design Engineer will be responsible for analyzing, designing, and detailing various structural systems and components to support the construction of building and infrastructure projects.
	This role requires a strong technical background in structural engineering principles, proficiency in design software, and the ability to collaborate effectively with multidisciplinary teams.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform structural analysis and design calculations for a variety of building and infrastructure projects
	Develop structural plans, details, and specifications in accordance with applicable building codes and engineering standards
	Utilize structural design software, such as ETABS, SAP2000, or RAM Structural System, to model and analyze complex structural systems
	Coordinate with architectural, civil, and MEP design teams to ensure the integration of structural elements within the overall project design
	Review and validate structural shop drawings and submittals from contractors and fabricators
	Collaborate with project managers, construction teams, and clients to address technical questions and resolve design-related issues
	Stay up-to-date with the latest building codes, design standards, and industry best practices
	Contribute to the continuous improvement of the firm&#39;s design processes and technical resources


Required Skills and Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s degree/ HND in Civil or Structural Engineering
	5+ years of experience in structural design, with a focus on building and/or infrastructure projects
	Professional Engineering (P.E.) license or the ability to obtain one within a reasonable timeframe
	Proficient in the use of structural design software, such as ETABS, SAP2000, or RAM Structural System
	Strong understanding of structural engineering principles, including load calculations, member design, and detailing
	Familiarity with building codes, design standards, and construction materials
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills
	Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and interface with project stakeholders
	Proficient in 3D modeling, BIM software, and CAD drafting


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 / Month

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to: recruitment@mshelconstruction.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Structural Design Engineer Job at Mshel Homes Limited




        
    

        
        Mshel Homes Limited is a real estate development and marketing company incorporated in 2018 with a unique vision to be the leading real estate company in Nigeria, with an outstanding reputation for honesty, integrity and impeccable customer service.

At Mshel Homes Limited our mission is to create a lifetime relationship with our clientele by deliverying property solutions through innovative and technological driven processes that caters to your real estate needs whilst adhering to the highest standards, systems and performance goals necessary to achieving your dreams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Structural Design Engineer

Job Location: Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced Structural Design Engineer to join our team. The Structural Design Engineer will be responsible for analyzing, designing, and detailing various structural systems and components to support the construction of building and infrastructure projects.
	This role requires a strong technical background in structural engineering principles, proficiency in design software, and the ability to collaborate effectively with multidisciplinary teams.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform structural analysis and design calculations for a variety of building and infrastructure projects
	Develop structural plans, details, and specifications in accordance with applicable building codes and engineering standards
	Utilize structural design software, such as ETABS, SAP2000, or RAM Structural System, to model and analyze complex structural systems
	Coordinate with architectural, civil, and MEP design teams to ensure the integration of structural elements within the overall project design
	Review and validate structural shop drawings and submittals from contractors and fabricators
	Collaborate with project managers, construction teams, and clients to address technical questions and resolve design-related issues
	Stay up-to-date with the latest building codes, design standards, and industry best practices
	Contribute to the continuous improvement of the firm's design processes and technical resources


Required Skills and Qualifications


	Bachelor's degree/ HND in Civil or Structural Engineering
	5+ years of experience in structural design, with a focus on building and/or infrastructure projects
	Professional Engineering (P.E.) license or the ability to obtain one within a reasonable timeframe
	Proficient in the use of structural design software, such as ETABS, SAP2000, or RAM Structural System
	Strong understanding of structural engineering principles, including load calculations, member design, and detailing
	Familiarity with building codes, design standards, and construction materials
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills
	Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and interface with project stakeholders
	Proficient in 3D modeling, BIM software, and CAD drafting


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 / Month

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
